page: Amazon Web Services x8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_tile and AMX_int8). It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the x8i.16xlarge demonstrates strong computational and memory performance. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2176.0 and a multi-core score of 20822.0, both placing in the top 10% tier. Its memory subsystem delivers a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 5104.32 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read bandwidth drops to an average tier of 173.07 GB/sec. The hardware-accelerated matrix extensions contribute to top-tier LLM inference speeds, processing 303.42 tokens per second for a medium 7B model prompt. Additionally, it handles high-throughput workloads efficiently, showing a static web serving throughput of 106.03 GB/sec and executing 113.79 million Redis SET operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8i.16xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound applications, leveraging fast DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU allocation to maximize throughput per resource unit. However, the absence of local NVMe storage requires external network-attached storage, which may introduce I/O latency tradeoffs. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale Redis caching layers, in-memory analytics, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but leverages Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. Benchmarks show top-tier performance, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 20822.0 and static web serving throughput of 106.03 GB/sec. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and prompt processing speeds for medium-sized LLM models. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive databases, large-scale caching, and in-memory analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance for databases and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 1024 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AMX and AVX-512 instruction support
Top-tier Geekbench multi-core and static web serving benchmark performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and in-memory analytics
